name: omarchy_shell_tools
content:
She Toos 
In addition to the standard Linux toos, Omarchy aso ships with a bunch of enhanced she
toos. Here are the key ones.
fzf 
fzf gives you fuzzy finding of fies via the ff  aias. Go to any directory, type ff , and
you' be abe to fuzzy find your way to any fie in that tree, whie seeing a preview of the
fies you're narrowing down on the right-hand side.
You can use Ctrl + R  to use fzf to fuzzy find through your command history.
This too is aso used by Neovim when you type Space Space .
The fu manua can be found via man fzf .
Zoxide 
Zoxide is a repacement for cd. It remembers the directories you've been in, so you can
more easiy jump to them next time. Say you do cd ~/.local/share/omarchy once. Next
time, you can just do cd omarchy  (or even just cd oma ), and Zoxide wi take you directy
there.
The fu manua can be found via man zoxide .
ripgrep 
ripgrep searches the contents of fies by using rg <pattern> <path>, ike rg Controller
app/ to find a mentions of Controller  in the directory app.
This too is aso used by Neovim when you type Space S G .
The fu manua can be found via man rg .
eza 
eza is a repacement for s. It gives you directory istings with more information, coor, and
icons. By defaut, eza has been aiased as s. You can aso use lt  to get a isting of two-
deep eves of nesting. lsa  gives you a isting incuding hidden fies. And lta  a nested
isting with hidden fies.
The fu manua can be found via man eza .
fd 
fd is an easier to use repacement for find . Use fd person.rb  to find a fie caed
 within the current tree.  wi search the entire fie system. 
Shell Tools · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/57/shell-tools
1 of 2 2/2/26, 1:24 AM

---

person.rb within the current tree. fd person.rb / wi search the entire fie system. fd
person.rb / -H searches the entire fie system, incuding hidden directories.
The fu manua can be found via man fd .
try 
try makes it easy to manage programming experiments with date-stamped directories. A
experiments ive in ~/Work/tries  and you can access them via try .
Shell Tools · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/57/shell-tools
